title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to Manufacturing Automation: Definition and importance, History
and evolution of automation in manufacturing, Benefits of automation, Types of
automation: Fixed, programmable, and flexible
automation, Reasons for automating, Automation strategies

title: Module 2
content:
Automated Flow Lines and Assembly Systems: Detroit Type Automation and
Flow Lines: Automated flow lines: Types and their selection, Methods of work part
transport: Continuous, intermittent, and asynchronous, Transfer mechanisms,
Controlling automated flow lines, Flow line performance analysis: Average
production time, production rate, and line efficiency, Partial automation: Reasons,
advantages, and drawbacks, Component quality control and effects of machine
jamming, Automated Assembly Systems: Historical developments of the assembly
process, Choice of assembly methods: Cost, production rate, labour availability, and
market life, Advantages of automatic assembly, Design for automated assembly,
Transfer systems: In-line and
rotary.

title: Module 3
content:
Feeders and Robotics in Manufacturing: Types of Feeders: Vibratory bowl
feeders: Mechanics and analysis, Effect of frequency, track acceleration, and
vibration angle, Non-vibratory feeders: Types (e.g., reciprocating tube hopper, center
board hopper, external gate hopper, rotary disk feeder), Analysis and design of part
orienting devices, feed tracks, and part placing mechanisms.
In-Bowl and Out-of-Bowl Toolings: Typical orienting systems: Wiper blade,
pressure break, slot in the track, Analysis of part orienting systems, Examples of
out-of-bowl toolings, Integration of feeders with automated systems

title: Module 4
content:
Safety and Implementation in Manufacturing Automation: Safety and
Standards in Manufacturing Automation: Safety protocols and risk assessment,
International standards (ISO, ANSI), Regulatory compliance, Implementation and
Integration of Automation Systems: Planning and design of automated systems,
Integration with existing manufacturing processes, Economic and Social Impacts
of Automation: Cost-benefit analysis, Impact
on labour and employment, Ethical considerations.
